task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium DICOM dataset is loaded and visible in the medical imaging software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The software interface (e.g., InVesalius) shows 2D axial, sagittal, and coronal slices of a human head/skull.

task_completion.description: Verify that a segmentation mask capturing the skin/soft tissue surface is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored overlay (segmentation mask) is visible on the 2D slices, specifically outlining the outermost boundary of the head/face (the skin surface), rather than just the bone.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 3D surface model of the external facial and cranial soft tissue contour is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer panel displays a 3D rendered model of a human face/head based on the soft tissue mask.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent initiates the export of the 3D surface as a PLY file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D Surface' or 'Save As' dialog box is open, with the file format explicitly set to PLY (*.ply).

task_completion.description: Verify that the PLY file is saved to the correct directory with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window or terminal shows the file 'skin_surface.ply' successfully created in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface of the medical imaging software to generate the surface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through menus, adjusting thresholds, and using the software's built-in 3D generation and export tools, rather than running a background script.

integrity.description: Verify the generated 3D model corresponds to the actual CT data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D surface in the viewer must look like a realistic human facial contour derived from medical scans, not a generic placeholder shape or downloaded model.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the software export by creating a fake or empty file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen using terminal commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/skin_surface.ply' or a text editor to create the final output file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/skin_surface.ply. The exported PLY file must be a genuine 3D surface with at least 10,000 vertices, 5,000 faces, and a file size greater than 300 KB. The DICOM series used is ct_cranium/0051.
